2604.09426.title: Three Modalities, Two Design Probes, One Prototype, and No Vision: Experience-Based Co-Design of a Multi-modal 3D Data Visualization Tool
2604.09426.paper_summary: Three-dimensional (3D) data visualizations, such as surface plots, are vital in STEM fields from biomedical imaging to spectroscopy, yet remain largely inaccessible to blind and low-vision (BLV) people. To address this gap, we conducted an Experience-Based Co-Design with BLV co-designers with expertise in non-visual data representations to create an accessible, multi-modal, web-native visualization tool. Using a multi-phase methodology, our team of five BLV and one non-BLV researcher(s) participated in two iterative sessions, comparing a low-fidelity tactile probe with a high-fidelity digital prototype. This process produced a prototype with empirically grounded features, including reference sonification, stereo and volumetric audio, and configurable buffer aggregation, which our co-designers validated as improving analytic accuracy and learnability. In this study, we target core analytic tasks essential for non-visual 3D data exploration: orientation, landmark and peak finding, comparing local maxima versus global trends, gradient tracing, and identifying occluded or partially hidden features. Our work offers accessibility researchers and developers a co-design protocol for translating tactile knowledge to digital interfaces, concrete design guidance for future systems, and opportunities to extend accessible 3D visualization into embodied data environments.
2604.09426.paper_summary_zh: 三維 (3D) 數據視覺化，例如表面圖，在從生物醫學影像到光譜學的 STEM 領域中至關重要，但對於盲人和低視力 (BLV) 人士來說仍然難以接觸。為了解決這一問題，我們與具有非視覺數據表示專業知識的 BLV 共同設計者進行了基於經驗的共同設計，以創建一個可訪問的、多模態的、網頁原生的視覺化工具。使用多階段的方法論，我們的五名 BLV 和一名非 BLV 研究者團隊參加了兩次迭代會議，將低保真觸覺探針與高保真數位原型進行比較。這一過程產生了一個具有實證基礎特徵的原型，包括參考聲音化、立體和體積音頻以及可配置的緩衝聚合，我們的共同設計者驗證了這些特徵能提高分析準確性和學習能力。在本研究中，我們針對非視覺 3D 數據探索的核心分析任務：定向、地標和峰值查找、比較局部極大值與全局趨勢、梯度追踪，以及識別被遮擋或部分隱藏的特徵。我們的工作為可訪問性研究者和開發者提供了一個共同設計協議，用於將觸覺知識轉換為數位介面，為未來系統提供具體的設計指導，並擴展可訪問的 3D 視覺化至具身數據環境的機會。
2604.09426.author: Sanchita S. Kamath et.al.
2604.09426.authors: Sanchita S. Kamath, Aziz N Zeidieh, Venkatesh Potluri, Sile O'Modhrain, Kenneth Perry, JooYoung Seo
